diagrams.title: Oracle Problem Visualization
diagrams.description: Shows the fundamental challenge: blockchain is isolated from external data sources. Smart contracts cannot directly access APIs, databases, sensors, or the internet. Smart Oracles act as bridges.
elements: External Data Sources (APIs, IoT sensors, databases)
Data flow arrows from external sources through oracle to blockchain
diagrams.purpose: Illustrate why oracles are necessary and the single point of connection between isolated blockchain and external world.

diagrams.title: Decentralized Oracle Architecture
diagrams.description: Shows how decentralized oracles prevent single points of failure. Multiple independent nodes retrieve data from multiple sources, aggregate results, and deliver to smart contract.
elements: Data Sources (multiple redundant sources per node)
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrate how decentralized oracles achieve trust through redundancy, consensus, and economic incentives.

diagrams.title: Oracle Data Flow Types
diagrams.description: Shows the difference between inbound (data into blockchain) and outbound (data out of blockchain) oracles with practical examples.
elements: Inbound Oracle: Weather API → Oracle → Weather Insurance Contract → Payout trigger
Outbound Oracle: Smart Contract → Oracle → Physical Door Lock → Door opens
diagrams.purpose: Clarify directional flow of data and when each oracle type is needed in blockchain applications.
